summary: Russell carries momentum from his Austrian masterclass into his home Grand Prix, seeking to capitalize on the confidence of a mature, controlled victory while Hamilton's pace-setting practice run signals Ferrari's competitive threat at a transformed Silverstone. McLaren and Red Bull will exploit the new car regulations' unpredictable grip characteristics around the high-speed corners, with pit-wall strategy becoming decisive as teams remain uncertain how tyres and setups will behave. A strong result for either Mercedes or Ferrari could shift the championship momentum decisively, with Russell eyeing his first home win and Hamilton hunting a record-extending ninth British Grand Prix triumph.
